title: Spectrum Outlines EntzuMC
description: Smooth animated outlines that bring a clean and dynamic spectrum effect to your Minecraft world.
body:
![Spectrum Outline EntzuMC](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/44a26e2d8a223a8c12f07b30162343e545eec18f_0.webp)
# Spectrum Outline EntzuMC

Upgrade your block highlights with vibrant moving colors and a sleek modern style.

---

## тЬи Features
- ЁЯМИ Animated spectrum outlines  
- ЁЯФД Flowing color loop around edges  
- ЁЯОи Custom color mode  
- ЁЯУП Adjustable thickness  
- тЪб Lightweight & optimized  
- ЁЯзй Vanilla shader based  

Perfect for PvP, building and cinematic gameplay.

---

## ЁЯЦе Compatibility
тЬФ Minecraft **1.21.x**  
тЬФ Vanilla  
тЬФ Iris  
тЬФ Sodium  
тЬФ OptiFine

---

## ЁЯУж Installation
1. Download the pack  
2. Move it to `.minecraft/resourcepacks`  
3. Activate it in game  

---

## тЭдя╕П Credits
Created by **EntzuMC**



# тЪЩя╕П Configuration Guide  
Spectrum Outline EntzuMC

All visual effects can be customized directly inside the shader files.

No extra mods or menus required тАФ just edit a few lines.

---

## ЁЯУВ Where to edit
Open the pack and go to:

assets/minecraft/shaders/core/

You will find:

- `rendertype_lines.vsh`
- `rendertype_lines.fsh`

At the top of both files you will see the configuration panel.

---

## ЁЯОо Changing the Mode

Find this line in **both files**:

#define MODE RAINBOW_LOOP

Replace `RAINBOW_LOOP` with one of these:

- `VANILLA` тЖТ Default Minecraft outline  
- `RAINBOW_FADE` тЖТ Smooth animated color shift  
- `RAINBOW_LOOP` тЖТ Colors travel around the edges  
- `CUSTOM` тЖТ Uses your selected color  
![Example](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/b597339e28a9d42f745b1988813036d210ea004f.png)

тЪая╕П The mode must match in both files.

---

## ЁЯУП Changing the Thickness

In `rendertype_lines.vsh`:

#define THICKNESS 2.5

Examples:
- `1.0` тЖТ vanilla  
- `2.0` тЖТ slightly thicker  
- `3.0` тЖТ bold  
- `4.0+` тЖТ very thick  

---

## ЁЯОи Custom Color Mode

If you use:

#define MODE CUSTOM

Change this in `rendertype_lines.fsh`:

#define CUSTOM_COLOR vec4(0.2, 0.8, 1.0, 1.0)

Format:
vec4(R, G, B, A)

Values go from **0.0 to 1.0**.

Examples:

White:
vec4(1.0, 1.0, 1.0, 1.0)

Red:
vec4(1.0, 0.0, 0.0, 1.0)

Purple:
vec4(0.7, 0.2, 1.0, 1.0)

---

## ЁЯМИ Changing Rainbow Speed

#define SPEED 2000.0

Higher = faster  
Lower = slower  

---

## ЁЯФБ Direction

#define DIRECTION 1.0

- `1.0` тЖТ normal  
- `-1.0` тЖТ reverse  

---

## ЁЯОЪ Smooth Transitions

#define SMOOTH true

- `true` тЖТ soft gradient  
- `false` тЖТ hard color steps  

---

## ЁЯТб Tips

тЬФ Restart Minecraft after editing  
тЬФ Use CUSTOM mode for quick testing  
тЬФ If nothing changes тЖТ make sure both files match  

---

Enjoy your personalized outlines ЁЯФе
